counter_argument: The primary security-pass skill gate still exists; H-09b/H-10b is the backstop for the no-skill path. But `git commit <path>` is a natural, common spelling (not exotic), it does commit worktree content, and the backstop's whole purpose is to catch exactly the un-gated path — so the bypass is real and the severity stands.
rationale: Confirmed by reading pre-bash.py 312-341: index-only `--cached` scan, worktree unioned only on -a/add. Real load-bearing-gate bypass via a natural spelling. Lead of group g1.

counter_argument: Same pathspec mechanism as appsec-001 applied to H-14; migration immutability is binding-by-digest so an edited migration could also slip. Slightly lower likelihood than the crypto/secret case but the same root cause.
rationale: Same root cause as appsec-001 (index-only scan vs pathspec-commit semantics), same file, fixed together. Combined into g1.

Same backstop region (pre-bash.py 312-341) and same hardening fix surface as appsec-001/002 — the H-09b/H-10b commit backstop. Distinct mechanism (fail-open on git-diff error vs pathspec bypass) but belongs in the same fix group g1; asymmetry with the fail-closed H-14 sibling is the tell.

Real: H-05 audit-log guard is purely lexical; `$f=overrides.log; rm $f` evades it. Low because it needs deliberate two-step indirection and the trail is partly reconstructable from git history, but it's a gap in an unrecoverable control worth recording. Lexical-only enforcement of an integrity control is the durable lesson.

Verified against _hooklib.py 41-51: CRYPTO_RE omits rc2 and blowfish, both explicitly forbidden by security-controls.md. Direct policy-vs-implementation contradiction, trivial S fix. Combined with secrets-002 (same file, same detection-regex-gap class) as g2.

Verified: leading `\b` in SECRET_RE won't fire inside compound `FARM_API_KEY`, and the project key prefix `sk-C…` matches none of AKIA/ghp_/sk-ant-. Net-new gap, distinct from the 2026-06-22 colon/object-form breadth fix. Combined with secrets-001 as g2.

Confirmed: farm.ts run() helper has no timeout/kill path while the API call does (AbortController). A hung gate/setup/mutation command wedges a worker slot and the scheduler never finalizes — defeats the walk-away design. The declared shell-trust exception covers untrusted-input, not liveness, so this is in scope.

Confirmed: defaultBuildImage ignores `docker create` (and `docker cp`) exit codes; a partial failure yields an empty checkout, degenerate dephash, and a wrong/colliding cache tag — a silent correctness bug. The finally already tears down the helper, so only the result checks are missing.

Control-flow sound (observed:false, inferred not executed): an early throw leaves integrationWorktree undefined, and the finally passes undefined into spawn argv → TypeError masks the real setup error. Narrow window, low blast radius (report already written), but a clean mechanical S fix. Calibrated just above the low-confidence bar on the strength of the control-flow reading.

Real and verifiable: 18 @img/sharp-libvips-* (LGPL-3.0-or-later) + tslib (0BSD) in site/package-lock.json are outside the approved-license list. But the response is a governance choice — approving LGPL-3.0 build-time deps is the same ADR-grade decision the user made via SMARTS for BlueOak-1.0.0/CC0-1.0 (per security-controls.md), not a mechanical fix. Routed to the user as a license-approval decision, with the docs-site/build-time (non-payload) scope as context. Not a fix-ticket.

Real (Math.random for container-name suffixes) but marginal: the lens's own analysis shows container names are not isolation identifiers (resolution is by label via registry.ts, not name), so this is not a security-controls.md CSPRNG-required context. Below the low-severity issue bar after calibration; preserved as a nice-to-have, not filed this run.

Confirmed: project_root() spawns `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` on every enforcement hook's first action; process creation is slow on Windows (~15-30ms) and this fires on every Bash/Write/Edit. statusline.py already has the .git-walk pattern to reuse. Highest-value perf finding — directly felt per interactive tool call.

counter_argument: Self-scored high, but security-controls.md is 8.9KB and OS page-cache makes 2-3 repeat reads within one short-lived hook process sub-millisecond on warm cache. Real and trivially fixable, but not high.
rationale: scope_globs/_read_controls re-reads controls per path-check; cache at module load / mtime-keyed. Combined with performance-002 (same _hooklib per-call hot-path, same module-load-precompute fix) as g6.

counter_argument: Self-scored high, but re.compile memoizes by pattern string (re._cache, size 512), so after the first call the 44 patterns are cheap cache hits, not recompiles — the agent acknowledged the memoization. Steady-state cost is far below the self-score. Low.
rationale: Pre-compile the constant default glob sets at module load. Combined with performance-001 as g6.

statusline renders on every tool-call completion; session_start does an O(N) scan+parse of session json files per render. Fast-path by PID or cache the resolved start in the ledger. Combined with performance-005 (same statusline per-render redundant reads) as g7.
arbiter_state reads 5 .codearbiter files per render with no caching, on the every-tool-call render path. mtime-keyed cache or per-render local. Combined with performance-004 as g7.

head_on_protected_tip spawns 3 sequential git rev-parse where one `git rev-parse HEAD main master` suffices. Detached-HEAD-on-commit is rare so impact is low, but it is a clean S fix. Confidence above the low bar; keep.

counter_argument: Self-scored high. The redactor erring means over-redaction (the safe direction for an outbound boundary), and no concrete secret class has been shown to be caught by the gate yet missed by the redactor. So present exploitable risk is lower than high implies — but it IS a security boundary maintained by two hand-synced patterns with a demonstrably false kept-in-step comment, so it remains a real keeper at medium.
rationale: farm.ts SECRET_LINE and _hooklib.SECRET_RE have already diverged on keyword set and match shape with only a prose comment as the sync mechanism. Fix = one shared must-match/must-not-match fixture asserted against both (CI-failing on divergence), or a single source of truth. Pairs with the g2 _hooklib regex work.

Confirmed: the container hardening argv (--user/--read-only/--cap-drop ALL/--security-opt no-new-privileges/--pids-limit/--memory/--cpus) is re-emitted verbatim in run.ts and claude-inside.ts. These flags ARE the isolation guarantee, and the token-bearing --with-claude box silently becomes the weaker of the two if a future flag lands in one only. Extract one shared hardeningFlags() helper + a parity test.

counter_argument: God-module findings risk being taste; but the evidence is concrete (5 subsystems that change for independent reasons, a security redactor sharing a file with experimental model-selection tooling) and the author already started extracting seams. Not taste. Lower priority though — an L behavior-preserving refactor.
rationale: Extract the two highest-value seams first (secret redactor 213-413, mutation engine 767-948), each with its own test; route via /ca:refactor with the vitest suite as parity proof. depends_on architecture-001 (the redactor extraction is where the shared fixture lands).

The append-only-log path set and the ADR-decisions path set are triplicated inline across pre-write/pre-edit/pre-bash — exactly the cross-hook drift _hooklib centralization was built to prevent. Adding an artifact (sprint-log.md was one) means editing 3+ files; a miss opens a guard hole. Lift to _hooklib as AUDIT_LOG_RE/is_audit_log + DECISIONS_PATH_RE. Natural home for the g5 H-05 hardening too.

statusline.py hand-rolls frontmatter/arbiter-enabled parsing that _hooklib.frontmatter_enabled/arbiter_active already encode (incl. the closed-block/malformed contract). The shared-lib mechanism is already used in statusline (it imports _taskboardlib), so reuse is available. Combined with architecture-007 as g8 (statusline.py behavior-preserving structural cleanup).

create.ts builds raw --mount type=volume argv as string literals in two places, bypassing the buildMountArgs single chokepoint that mounts.ts advertises and run.ts/cp.ts/claude-inside.ts honor. Safe today (volume names are internally generated, no bind), but the documented isolation invariant is literally false. Route create.ts through buildMountArgs, or correct the docstring.

~290 LOC stateful cost-ledger subsystem (pricing table, transcript accumulation, JSON persistence, sparkline) embedded in the statusline render entry-point, against coding-standards.md thin-entry-point rule. Pricing/dedup logic is drift-prone and untestable in isolation. Extract to _ledgerlib.py with a test, mirroring _metricslib/_taskboardlib. Combined with architecture-005 as g8.

Confirmed: taskwrite.py truncate-then-write of open-tasks.md (read-modify-write, no temp+rename). A crash between truncate and write-complete leaves the board empty; the board drives SessionStart + statusline in-flight counts with no auto-recovery. Fix = write .tmp then os.replace(). Combined with migration-002 (same non-atomic-write pattern, same fix) as g9.

migration-pass.py and security-pass.py write digest markers non-atomically. Correctness is preserved (a partial marker reads as uncovered, backstop stays fail-closed), so the only cost is a spurious gate re-run. Same os.replace() fix as migration-001. Combined as g9.

pre-edit.py H-05 guard: new.startswith(old) is unconditionally True when old_string is empty (Python), so an Edit with empty old_string on an audit log is not blocked at the hook layer. Distinct mechanism from appsec-003 (pre-bash variable indirection) — both H-05 bypasses, grouped in the plan under g5 (H-05 audit-log guard hardening). Confidence 0.8 (Edit tool empty-old_string handling unconfirmed); fix = block empty old_string on audit logs outright.

farm.ts validate() dereferences t.test.path / t.gate.commands / t.filesInScope without null guards; a plan.json with a null required field throws an opaque TypeError instead of a named validation error. Fail-closed outcome is correct (no task runs), so it is a DX/diagnosability low. Belongs with the farm.ts robustness work (g3). Add named-field guards.

counter_argument: It is a test gap, not a live bug, and H-09b exercises the SHARED commit-backstop block end-to-end — only the SECRET_RE-trigger branch and the secret-specific kind/tag/skill selection are untested, so the common diff-extraction/marker/block logic IS covered. That lowers it from 'the gate is unproven' to 'one branch of the gate is unproven'. But appsec-001 and secrets-001/002 prove this exact area harbors real bugs that tests didn't catch, so a high-value integration hole on the product's core secret lane is the right read.
rationale: Confirmed against test_hook_guards.py: H-09b crypto commit is tested end-to-end (6a-6e) but no test stages a SECRET_RE literal and drives git commit to assert [H-10b]. Combined with coverage-002 (same test file, same commit-gate integration coverage) as g11.

The `git commit -a/-am` worktree-diff branch of H-09b/H-10b (pre-bash.py 313-315) has no integration test, though the H-14 migration backstop has the analogous OB-10 -a case. A COMMIT_ALL_RE regression would let `git commit -am` ship crypto/secret changes. Combined with coverage-001 as g11. Note: this pairs naturally with the g1 pathspec-bypass fix — add the -a AND pathspec cases together.

validateRepoUrl's scp-like double-colon (`host::address` transport-helper) rejection has no pinning test in create.test.ts. Correct today via the `[^:]` guard, but this is the git-arg-injection boundary on untrusted clone input — a regex edit could silently re-open it. A negative pinning test is cheap insurance on a security boundary. Keep.

is_ci_path/is_deploy_path custom extend/exclude declaration parsing (scope_globs _CI_DECL_RE/_DEPLOY_DECL_RE) is untested, though is_migration_path's equivalent grammar is (OB-02/03). Advisory gates (H-15/H-16), so low, but a broken custom-scope parse silently drops the advisory. Clean S fix mirroring the migration tests. Keep.

post-write-edit H-12 governed-path reminder has unit coverage of governs_index() but no integration test of the full hook path (write governed file -> [H-12] in stderr). Advisory-only so low, and confidence just above the bar. Clean S fix. Keep.

Confirmed: session-start.py removes a live dev-active marker with no DEV:exit append, leaving overrides.log with an orphaned DEV:enter; arbiter.md prose asks the LLM to repair this 'at the next opportunity' — prose-only enforcement of a first-class audit-trail concern. Fix = append a synthetic 'DEV:exit BY: session-cleanup' before removing the marker. Clean S, no friction. Keep.

Real and refined (scopes the gap to /sprint auto-decisions + /dev entry), but this is the 'compel a log write' enforcement-strategy decision the user ALREADY deferred as a separate design call (open-questions.md line 19) — a hook that forces an audit write is the invasive 'test-running commit hook' class. The response is an ADR-grade choice, not a mechanical fix → decision-required, with a recommendation to promote it to a tracked CONFIRM-NN. The no-regrets sub-action (document the integrity-vs-completeness distinction in security-controls.md Audit trail) can ride along regardless of the decision.

farm runTask crash handler writes 'crashed: <message>' to farm-results.jsonl with no run-id correlation (two concurrent runs interleave indistinguishably) and no stack trace (call site lost). Task id is present, so attribution is partial. Low-effort diagnosability win: mint a run-id at main() and include err.stack (truncated). Belongs with the g3 farm.ts robustness work.

defaultCloneRepo's spawnAsync uses stdio:'ignore', discarding git stderr; a failed clone surfaces only 'clone of <url> failed (exit 1)' with no actionable cause (network/auth/disk). The observability twin of reliability-002 (unchecked docker create) — same create.ts failure-surfacing weakness. Combined into g4 (ca-sandbox build/clone failure handling). Capture a bounded slice of stderr into the thrown error.

security-controls.md Audit trail claims the H-05 guards enforce append-only 'at every tool-call boundary', but the pre-bash.py code comment itself documents known regex gaps (exec N>file, >>>, process-substitution) as accepted residual risk. The code is honest; the policy doc overstates coverage. Pure doc-accuracy fix (no code change), belongs in the g5 H-05 group. Keep.

parseChatCompletion does `JSON.parse(text) as typeof data` at the third-party Zen API trust boundary with no runtime shape check; an unexpected outer shape (no choices array) returns ok:true content:'' and silently exhausts retries — the exact misconfig failure mode the function was created to make actionable. Distinct from the architecture JSON.parse-as-Plan finding (different parse site). Add an outer-shape guard returning ok:false. Belongs with g3 farm.ts robustness.

mutationCheck JSON.parse(j[0]) as {score?} with no object guard; a FARM_MUTATION_CMD emitting 'score' inside a string/number parses to a non-object, parsed.score is undefined, mutationCheck silently returns null (mutation guard disabled) with no diagnostic. Add a typeof-object guard + optional stderr note. g3 area. Keep low.

mutationCheck writes originals.get(c.file)! with a non-null assertion; the invariant holds today (candidates derive from impl=originals keyset) but is type-invisible. A future generateMutants emitting a candidate for a non-original file would write the literal string 'undefined' into the worktree. Replace ! with an explicit guard that preserves the file on a miss. g3 area. Keep low.

counter_argument: Self-scored medium invoking the 'never raise on malformed user input' rule, but that rule governs hook-stdin paths, not internal library logic (the lens itself says so in dx-005). set_state is called from the /ca:task path with controlled state values, so the KeyError is defensive-only, not a live crash today. Hence low, not medium.
rationale: set_state's `state` value contract ({queued,in_progress,done}) is undocumented and unguarded (_MARK_BY_STATE[state] -> KeyError on anything else). Defensive guard + docstring. Combined with dx-005 (same file, same undocumented-value-contract class) as g10.

promote()'s `mode` has a silent fallthrough: any non-'interactive' value (incl. a typo) takes the auto branch and MUTATES persistent state (open-tasks.md/open-questions.md) with no diagnostic. Slightly worse than dx-004 (silent state mutation vs crash), but still low. Guard unknown modes (raise/ValueError or warn). Combined with dx-004 as g10.

counter_argument: Initially appeared to conflict with the reliability lens ('network.ts hard-errors on unknown policy') and security-controls.md ('unknown policy is a hard error'). Verified: those describe network.ts applyNetworkPolicy, which DOES use a closed NetworkPolicy union with a never-exhaustive default that throws — correct. dx-006 is a DIFFERENT layer: run.ts buildRunArgs (T-06) string-compares the OPEN NetPolicy and emits --network none only for exact 'offline'. So the real, narrow risk is a typo of 'offline' itself degrading open at T-06 — not a broad 'grants network'. No doc conflict.
rationale: run.ts NetPolicy='offline'|(string&{}); buildRunArgs line 125 only airgaps exact 'offline'. A typo ('Offline') silently drops --network none at the T-06 layer. Narrow the safe-default handling (closed union for handled policies, or a normalized/checked compare) so a typo can't silently lose the airgap. Keep low.

coding-standards.md requires the public API listed as `name(args)->type` in each lib header; _taskboardlib.py and _metricslib.py comply but _hooklib.py and _sloplib.py omit it entirely, despite _hooklib being the surface every untrusted-payload hook routes through. Verifiable standards violation, clean S doc fix. Keep low.
